hero.title: Pebble Beach Beneath
hero.description: Plage d'Étretat sits on Normandy's Côte d'Albâtre, famed for its white chalk cliffs, natural arches ("Gates"), and the iconic "Needle" rock formation.

overview.title: What You'll See Here
overview.desc: From the "clack-clack" of pebbles to three natural stone arches, Étretat's coastline packages geology with literature, painting, and sea breeze.
overview.cliffs_desc: White chalk cliffs eroded by the sea form natural arches; the most famous are typically called Porte d'Aval / Porte d'Amont.
overview.pebble_desc: The beach is made of pebbles, making walking harder; but that's exactly why it's better to "slow down" and listen to the waves.
overview.tides_desc: At low tide you can explore the reefs and cave areas; but high tide may trap you between two sections of cliffs.

photos.title: Photo Gallery
photos.desc: Following the rhythm of Port of Honfleur: few but refined images, with clear captions and navigation.

tips.title: How to Enjoy It More
tips.desc: Split your visit into 3 parts: Beach → Viewpoints → Back to town. The core rule: 'Check the tides, leave buffer time.'
tips.step1_title: First, find your rhythm at the beach
tips.step1_desc: Walk to the open viewing area, observe the wave line, wind direction, and crowd flow.
tips.step2_desc: If you plan to get close to the arches/reefs, you must check the tide schedule in advance.
tips.step3_title: Climb to cliff trails for panoramic views
tips.step3_desc: From above, the coastline's arc and light changes are most beautiful, especially at sunset.
tips.safety_desc: Walking toward the arches, high tide may block your return path. The safest approach: only enter during low tide windows.
tips.rule_desc: Pebbles are the coastline's natural "shock absorbers" that reduce storm surge impact on embankments.

map.title: Location & Map
map.desc: Interactive map and directions to Port of Honfleur.
map.golden_hour: Golden Hour — Evening blue hour brings the harbor's mirror-like reflections to life. The Vieux Bassin becomes a perfect canvas of light and architecture.
map.photo_spot: Photo Spot — Low angle shots along the waterfront capture stunning water mirrors. Best viewpoints: Lieutenance terrace, Mont Joli panorama, Jetée walkway.
map.route_plan: Route Planning — Click to plan your route from anywhere to the port. Avoid peak parking by choosing Môle lot (10 min walk, €6-8).
map.parking_tip: Peak hours: Môle parking (10 min walk, €6-8) recommended over central lots.
map.open_hours_desc: Public waterfront; lifeguards may be present in summer. Check local notices on arrival.
map.best_light_desc: Sunset hour paints the limestone cliffs in warm amber tones. Arrive 1 hour before sunset.

sources.title: Data & References
sources.desc: To keep information credible, we only cite publicly accessible pages.

reviews.title: What Visitors Say
reviews.desc: Real reviews from Google Maps travelers who have experienced the beach.
reviews.disclaimer: Rating and review data sourced from Google Maps (last updated: 2026). We display a selection of verified high-rated reviews. For the complete and latest reviews, click the link below.
reviews.reviewer1_content: This is a very pleasant small town. The buildings around the dock are very beautiful and interesting. There are restaurants, cafés, art galleries everywhere, and of course souvenir shops. There's a market on Saturday mornings. There's an interesting wooden church in the center. Definitely worth a visit.
reviews.reviewer2_content: This is a really worthwhile place to visit. We came by cruise ship so only had one day to visit, but it was really beautiful. There's lots to do and see, especially the wooden church (Saint Catherine's Church) and the Chapel of Grace with its bells. We also enjoyed oysters, whelks and shrimp while enjoying a drink overlooking the Vieux Bassin.
reviews.reviewer3_content: Beautiful harbor town with incredible architecture and charm. The reflections on the water at sunset are magical. We spent hours walking around the old harbor and took hundreds of photos.
reviews.reviewer4_content: Our favorite spot in Normandy. The wooden church is unique and the art galleries are wonderful. The seafood restaurants by the harbor serve incredibly fresh fish. A must-visit!
reviews.reviewer5_content: Perfect location for photography. The low tide creates amazing mirror reflections. I recommend visiting during blue hour for the best shots of the harbor.
